license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.gptbvnn
title: Lysate Preparation from Viruses in Cultured Cells Growing in Suspension and Lifted Cells
author: Bioline

steps_raw.instruction: Transfer cell suspension to a 1.5 mL RNase-free microcentrifuge tube (user supplied). The steps for preparing the lysate are different depending on the starting material. Please ensure you follow the correct procedure for your starting material (see the different lysate protocols here ). The subsequent steps (detailed here ) for Genomic DNA Removal and Total RNA Purification are the same in all cases.
Centrifuge at no more than 200 x g for 10 min to pellet cells. 00:10:00

Carefully decant the supernatant to ensure that the pellet is not dislodged. Ensure that a few microliters of media are left behind with the pellet in order to ensure that the pellet is not dislodged.

Add 350 μL of Lysis Buffer RX to the pellet. Lyse cells by vortexing for 15s. Ensure entire pellet is completely dissolved before proceeding to the next step. 00:00:15 Note: For input amounts greater than 10 6 cells, it is recommended that the lysate is passed through a nuclease-free 20 gauge (0.9 mm) syringe needle 5-10 times, in order to reduce the viscosity of the lysate prior to loading onto the column.
Proceed to Genomic DNA removal and total RNA purification from all types of lysate .
